evals.prompt: Publish a blog post from the research findings in $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings blog $(pwd)/artifact.json` to produce artifact.json, then `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $(pwd)/artifact.json blog $(pwd)/blog.md` to render it. Confirm zero citation leaks before reporting done.
evals.expected_output: blog.md with a # title, lede paragraph, ## section headings, and ## Sources; artifact.json valid JSON with .newsworthiness and at least 3 sections; no internal MIF tokens (urn:mif:, kg-copier-0001, extensions.harness, reports/_meta) in blog.md.

deterministic_checks.description: blog.md output file was created
artifact.json is valid JSON
artifact carries a newsworthiness signal
artifact has one section per surviving finding (3 total)
blog.md opens with a # title line
blog.md has a ## Sources section
public primary source URL appears in Sources
MIF URNs do not leak into the published post
internal finding IDs do not leak into the published post
harness extension keys do not leak into the published post
internal corpus paths do not leak into the published post
internal finding ID handle pattern (f_<dim>_<n>) does not appear in blog

expectations: The blog.md file reads as a standalone publishable post with a clear title, an opening lede paragraph without any section heading, and named ## subheadings for the body sections.
Every claim in the body traces to a source listed in the ## Sources section; no citation appears in the body that is absent from the Sources list.

The artifact.json file contains the @type Artifact envelope with finding_refs, sections (one per finding), and a deduplicated sources array — demonstrating the typed contract is intact before rendering.

evals.prompt: I need to verify that falsified findings are excluded from published blog posts. Please do the following: (1) Create a temporary findings directory at /tmp/eval-blog-e2-findings/ and copy the three sample findings from $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/ into it. (2) Create a fourth file /tmp/eval-blog-e2-findings/finding-falsified.json with the exact JSON shape of finding-copier.json but with these changes: set extensions.harness.verification.verdict to 'falsified', set title to 'FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ title that must not publish', set content to 'FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ content that must be excluded from any published output.', set @id to 'urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-falsified-9999'. (3) Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh /tmp/eval-blog-e2-findings blog $(pwd)/artifact.json`. (4) Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $(pwd)/artifact.json blog $(pwd)/blog.md`. Confirm the falsified sentinel text does not appear in either artifact.json or blog.md.
evals.expected_output: artifact.json and blog.md both produced; the string 'FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ' is absent from both files; blog.md still contains ## Sources and the surviving public source URLs; artifact.json still has 3 sections (only the 3 surviving findings).

deterministic_checks.description: blog.md was produced despite the falsified finding
artifact.json is valid JSON
falsified finding's sentinel text is absent from the artifact
falsified finding's sentinel text is absent from the rendered blog
surviving findings still produce a Sources section
artifact has exactly the 3 surviving sections, not 4
no MIF URNs appear in the published blog

expectations: The falsified finding is completely absent from all output — not summarised, not hedged, not mentioned as excluded; it simply does not appear.
The blog and artifact continue to reflect the three surviving findings faithfully, with sources traceable to copier.readthedocs.io and cookiecutter.readthedocs.io.

evals.prompt: Publish a blog post from the research in $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings using the 'technical' synthesis genre. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings technical $(pwd)/artifact.json` then `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $(pwd)/artifact.json blog $(pwd)/blog.md`. Report done when both files are written.
evals.expected_output: artifact.json with genre='technical'; blog.md identical in structure to the blog genre — title, lede, ## content headings, ## Sources — and citation-leak clean: no urn:mif:, no internal IDs, copier.readthedocs.io present in Sources.

deterministic_checks.description: blog.md was produced from the technical-genre artifact
artifact.json is valid JSON
artifact.json records the requested synthesis genre
blog render always produces a ## Sources section regardless of synthesis genre
public primary source URL survives into the Sources section
MIF URNs do not appear in the blog regardless of synthesis genre
internal finding ID pattern is absent from the blog for any genre
blog.md starts with a # title regardless of synthesis genre

expectations: The blog.md output is structurally identical to the blog-genre variant — the blog render channel ignores the synthesis genre metadata and always produces title + lede + ## sections + ## Sources.
The artifact.json genre field is 'technical', confirming the genre parameter was passed through correctly to the synthesis step.

evals.prompt: Turn the research from $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings into a publishable blog post. Run `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings blog $(pwd)/artifact.json` then `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $(pwd)/artifact.json blog $(pwd)/blog.md`. The blog must have a clear title, structured ## body sections (not just the Sources heading), and a ## Sources list with properly formatted links.
evals.expected_output: blog.md with a # title on line 1 matching '# Findings: harness/example-topic', at least two ## headings (body sections plus ## Sources), source links in '- [title](url)' format, and cookiecutter.readthedocs.io in the Sources list.

deterministic_checks.description: title line matches the expected 'Findings: <namespace>' format
blog.md has at least two ## headings (a body section and ## Sources)
exact primary source URL appears in the Sources list
source title for the Cookiecutter source appears in the Sources list
harness extension metadata does not bleed into the rendered blog
internal finding ID for the distribution finding does not appear in the blog

expectations: The ## Sources section contains exactly two entries — 'Cookiecutter documentation' and 'Copier — Updating a project' — because the three findings share one URL (copier.readthedocs.io) which is deduplicated.
The body of the blog reads as a coherent narrative about template update propagation, not as a raw dump of finding IDs or MIF metadata fields.
